justification: All pass criteria met: FulfillmentWorker Kafka trace break clearly identified (ignores trace ID, generates new one). NotificationService and AuditService missing trace context both identified with specific integration edges. Consequence explicitly named — on-call engineer must manually correlate across 5 services, turning a 2-minute trace lookup into 30-minute log archaeology. Cross-service SLO gap identified (no end-to-end order latency metric, no p99 order-to-fulfillment tracking). Specific fix recommendations provided for each break (extract from Kafka headers, propagate in outbound HTTP calls). Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Identified all 4 broken integration edges with precise descriptions of why each breaks the trace chain
Consequence analysis is vivid and operationally grounded — describes the exact 3am debugging workflow an on-call engineer would face
Cross-service SLO gap called out explicitly with concrete examples (p99 order-to-fulfillment, end-to-end order latency)
Went beyond rubric requirements by identifying Kafka consumer lag invisibility, missing retry/circuit-breaker patterns, and timeout gaps
Escalations are well-routed — trace propagation fixes to Shaq, infrastructure gaps to Coach K
Confidence section honestly flags assumptions about service mesh and auto-instrumentation that could change the assessment
